content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council recorded £355.2 million in total spending across 30,580 transactions between 2021/22 and 2025/26.
  • The payment type for all transactions was categorised as Unknown, accounting for 100.0% of the total spend.
  • The council's spending data does not disclose any specific payment methods or types beyond the Unknown category.

Understanding Burnley's Payment Categories

Burnley Borough Council's published spending data for the period 2021/22 to 2025/26 reveals a notable gap in the categorisation of payment types. The entire £355.2 million spent across 30,580 transactions is listed under the Unknown category. This lack of specificity makes it challenging to analyse the council's spending patterns in detail.

The absence of detailed payment type information is a significant oversight. Typically, councils categorise payments into types such as invoices, grants, salaries, or procurement. However, Burnley's data does not provide this level of granularity. This makes it difficult for residents and stakeholders to understand how public funds are being allocated and spent.

The Implications of Unknown Payment Types

The categorisation of all payments as Unknown raises questions about transparency and accountability. Transparency in public spending is crucial for ensuring that taxpayer money is used efficiently and effectively. When payment types are not specified, it becomes harder for the public to scrutinise spending decisions and hold the council accountable.

For instance, knowing whether a significant portion of the £355.2 million was spent on procurement, grants, or salaries would provide valuable insights into the council's priorities and financial management. Without this information, it is difficult to assess whether the council is meeting its financial obligations and delivering value for money.

Comparing Burnley's Data with Transparency Standards

According to the Local Government Transparency Code, councils are required to publish detailed spending data to ensure transparency and accountability. Burnley Borough Council's data falls short of these standards by not specifying payment types. This lack of detail can hinder public trust and make it more difficult for residents to engage with the council's financial decisions.

To improve transparency, Burnley Borough Council could consider implementing a more detailed categorisation system for its spending data. This would not only comply with the Transparency Code but also provide residents with a clearer picture of how their taxes are being spent. For example, specifying whether payments are for services, goods, or grants would be a step in the right direction.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council asking for a breakdown of payment types for the period 2021/22 to 2025/26.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to raise questions about the lack of detail in the spending data.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see if more detailed information about payment types is available there.
  • Write to your local councillor expressing your concerns about the lack of transparency in Burnley's spending data and requesting more detailed categorisation.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a better understanding of how Burnley Borough Council allocates its funds.
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
